title: Feature: Re-home scout's frontend onto the shared app-platform
summary: Move scout from a self-contained Go app with an embedded HTML UI to a platform app: document its API as a stable contract, point its docs at the platform plan, rebuild its UI as a toolkit-built PWA (Go /api/* unchanged), and put it behind the shared Caddy/SSO edge so it inherits HTTPS, Google sign-in, and installability. Source of truth: brainbot/docs/app-platform.md + scout/docs/north-star.md.
outOfScope: Changing scout's Go pipeline (ingest/filter/enrich/verdict/triage) or its /api/* behavior — this is a frontend re-home, not a backend rewrite
Building the web-toolkit itself (that is brainbot FEAT-20260607_155517-4fd1 US-002/US-003 — a hard prerequisite for US-003 here)
Provisioning the VPS / running the deploy (the compose + vhost are authored here; standing up the box is separate ops)
Multi-user auth logic in scout (auth lives at the edge; scout only reads the injected identity header)
technicalConsiderations: Current UI is a single ~3,920-line internal/web/index.html embedded via go:embed; the Go server (internal/web/server.go) already serves every view via /api/* JSON, so the swap is frontend-only
PREREQUISITE: the validated web-toolkit from brainbot (FEAT-...-4fd1 US-002 extracted, US-003 validated) must exist before US-003 here
Toolkit brain client expects an app-side /api/brain/* read-only proxy; scout already has internal/brainbot for the profile panel to build on
Edge identity header: oauth2-proxy injects X-Auth-Request-Email (see brainbot compose/Caddyfile); scout exposes it as /api/me
Scout's invariant 5 (web-first) and the go:embed model in north-star.md must be updated to reference the platform direction
